taxonomy: Campylospermum glaberrimum is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae. The family Ochnaceae is a part of the larger clade of flowering plants known as angiosperms. Angiosperms are further classified into two main groups: monocots and dicots, with Ochnaceae being classified as dicots. Dicots are characterized by having two cotyledons in their seeds, which is a key characteristic used in their classification. The classification of Campylospermum glaberrimum reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the family Ochnaceae.
